column6: mojo_behavior
column7: mojito_behavior
column9: notes

column6: Indentation delimits suites and continuation rules suppress logical newlines.
column7: Lexer implements indentation, dedentation, continuations, comments, and bracket suppression.

column6: Supports integer, floating, boolean, string, byte, and interpolated literal families.
column7: Runs core numeric, boolean, and string literals; parses t-strings but rejects their semantics.
column9: Byte and richer literal families remain incomplete.

column6: Identifiers include keyword stropping for names that would otherwise be reserved.
column7: Identifiers and stropped names parse, check, mangle, and execute.

column6: Runtime statements are forbidden at file scope; declarations and compile-time bindings are allowed.
column7: Production compilation rejects executable file-scope statements.
column9: Legacy phase tests may opt into a non-conforming snippet mode.

column6: `def` declares functions and methods with typed arguments and optional result types.
column7: Ordinary `def` functions and methods check and execute.

column6: The removed legacy `fn` keyword is rejected; `def` is the sole function-declaration keyword.
column7: Legacy `fn` declarations are rejected by the parser.
column9: Compatibility is rejection parity, not an implementation target.

column6: Supports positional-only, keyword-only, default, and keyword arguments.
column7: Shared call binding supports `/`, `*`, defaults, and keywords.

column6: Homogeneous variadic positional and keyword collectors bind repeated values.
column7: Homogeneous free-function `*args` and `**kwargs` run.
column9: Method and generic keyword collectors remain narrower.

column6: Type packs permit heterogeneous variadic arguments with per-element types and compile-time iteration.
column7: Generic `*args: *ArgTypes` declarations infer literal/constructed argument types, check every element against the pack bound, expose compile-time length/index iteration, and execute through a type-erased collection.
column9: Index expressions carry the common pack bound; arbitrary-expression inference and per-index concrete type reflection remain open.

column6: Ordinary returns transfer an owned value and may apply implicit conversion.
column7: Typed owned returns, literal coercion, recursion, and path checking run.
column9: Return checking is intentionally conservative.

column6: Reference returns carry origins tying the result to caller-owned storage.
column7: Origin-checked reference returns, unions, projections, and escape rejection run.
column9: Safe caller-owned subset.

column6: Named `out` results support caller-transparent direct construction of nonmovable values.
column7: A single named result is excluded from caller argument binding, checked for initialization, and returned directly; constructor `out self` also runs.
column9: Definite initialization is conservative pending lifecycle-flow completion.

column6: Overload selection minimizes implicit conversions, then prefers nonvariadic candidates, shorter signatures, and applicable specialization tie-breaks.
column7: Functions and methods rank lexicographically by conversion count, including unique nonraising `@implicit` constructors, variadic use, signature length, and generic tie-break; convention-invalid candidates are filtered and unresolved ties remain errors.
column9: Generic-target implicit conversion and the full specialization lattice remain open.

column6: Struct methods may be declared static and called without a receiver.
column7: User-defined static methods check, overload, lower, and execute through the shared default/keyword call ABI without synthesizing a receiver.
column9: Shared call binding applies equally to static methods.

column6: Functions have signature types; current Mojo specializes generic functions through compile-time aliases before using them as callables.
column7: Non-capturing function values inhabit function-typed variables, parameters, and fields; an expected monomorphic function type contextually specializes a generic function value, and indirect calls retain raising effects.
column9: Mojito's runtime generic-function coercion is broader than current Mojo alias spelling; overloaded callable values and captures remain open.

column6: `var` supports explicit or inferred types and later assignment preserves type.
column7: Typed, inferred, and var-less introductions check and run with stable binding identity.

column6: Typed variables may be declared uninitialized and must be definitely initialized before use.
column7: Runtime variables currently require an initializer at declaration; selected out-self initialization is handled separately.

column6: Implicitly declared variables have function scope while explicit `var` bindings have block scope.
column7: Explicit bindings have block scope; var-less introduction is supported but does not yet implement the complete function-scope rule.

column6: Bindings use lexical scope and may shadow in nested scopes.
column7: Lexical scopes, same-scope redeclaration errors, and stable IDs under shadowing run.

column6: Standard scalar structs include sized integers, floating types, Bool, and SIMD width-one aliases.
column7: Core Int, UInt, Bool, Float64, sized numeric aliases, wrapping, and conversions run.
column9: The full dtype and literal-conversion matrix is incomplete.

column6: String and string-slice APIs provide Unicode-aware storage, formatting, and protocol integration.
column7: VM strings support literals, concatenation, equality, membership, length, and pragmatic conversion.
column9: No self-hosted Unicode-complete String.

column6: String literals include raw, multiline, adjacent, and lazy interpolated TString forms.
column7: Quoted and multiline strings plus escapes run; t-strings parse only, and raw/adjacent literal behavior is incomplete.

column6: Heterogeneous tuples support compile-time indexing, unpacking, packs, and iteration facilities.
column7: Tuple construction, constant indexing, unpacking, coercion, and value semantics run.
column9: General tuple packs and runtime indexing are rejected.

column6: Standard List, Dict, Set, Optional, and related generic collections are available.
column7: List literals plus bundled self-hosted List, Dict, Set, HashDict, HashSet, and Optional proof subsets run.
column9: Not API-compatible with the full Mojo standard library.

column6: List, set, and dictionary displays plus comprehensions construct inferred collection types.
column7: Homogeneous nonempty list displays run; set/dict displays and comprehensions are not implemented.

column6: Slice syntax supports omitted bounds, steps, and standard slice semantics across supporting types.
column7: Slice syntax and a narrow executable subset run.
column9: Complete slicing remains open.

column6: `Variant` stores one of a compile-time list of types.
column7: No general Variant type.

column6: Operator syntax dispatches through dunder methods and standard numeric protocols.
column7: Core arithmetic, comparison, boolean, membership, unary, power, floor division, and augmented forms run.
column9: Bitwise, shifts, matrix multiply, and parts of the protocol surface remain absent.

column6: Comparisons chain and short-circuit while evaluating operands in order.
column7: Chained comparisons and boolean short-circuiting run.

column6: `if`/`elif`/`else` and conditional expressions branch on Boolable conditions.
column7: Statements and ternary expressions lower through HIR/MIR and run.
column9: Builtin Boolable coverage is narrower than the full stdlib.

column6: `while` and protocol-driven `for` loops support break and continue.
column7: While, range loops, list iteration, and user iterator protocol run with break/continue.
column9: Reference loop bindings and the complete iterator surface remain open.

column6: `while` and `for` may run an `else` suite after normal exhaustion but not after break or return.
column7: Loop `else` clauses are not represented or executed.

column6: Destructuring and pattern forms bind structured values.
column7: Tuple unpacking assignments run; richer patterns are rejected.

column6: Errors propagate through `raise` and are handled by try/except/else/finally.
column7: Builtin and user-defined typed error values propagate through VM frames and structured handlers without losing their fields.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Raising functions and calls participate in checked `raises` effects.
column7: `raise` is restricted to a compatible `raises` body or protected `try`; selected free-function/method overloads and indirect calls must be handled or propagated.
column9: Trait-requirement effect coverage remains part of broader trait completion.

column6: Errors may be typed and parameterized, including Never interactions.
column7: Typed errors, inferred handler bindings, parametric callable errors, `Never` bottom coercion, and nonraising `raises Never` are checked.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Context managers use `with`, `__enter__`, and `__exit__` protocols.
column7: Syntax parses; semantic checking rejects it.

column6: Structs define stored fields, methods, parameters, and declared trait conformance.
column7: Fields, methods, generic parameters, associated facts, and nominal conformance run.

column6: Struct values are independent under copy and transfer under move.
column7: Struct copy/move behavior and nested value semantics run.

column6: Source modules and packages provide lexical namespaces, imports, `__init__.mojo` re-exports, and public wildcard imports; executable top-level initialization is forbidden.
column7: Selective, wildcard, dotted, dots-only relative, aliased, and block-scoped imports link with declaration provenance and lexical shadowing; package initializers re-export declarations.
column9: Compiled `.mojoc` artifacts remain outside the source linker.

column6: `import module` and aliases create qualified module namespaces while declarations in different modules retain distinct identity.
column7: Aliased and unaliased dotted qualified calls, values, and types resolve to module-qualified internal symbols, isolating same-named declarations and overloads.
column9: General first-class module values remain outside the flattened architecture.

column6: Argument conventions distinguish default read, mut, var, ref, out, and deinit access with access rules based on transfer, copying, and aliasing.
column7: Current `var` consuming syntax runs, removed `owned` syntax is rejected, and Copyable reads are materialized before overlapping exclusive arguments while non-Copyable aliases remain rejected.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Ownership transfer, use-after-move prevention, partial moves, and reinitialization are checked.
column7: CFG ownership analysis covers moves, partial moves, branches, loops, calls, and reinitialization.

column6: Borrowed references extend owner lifetime and enforce shared-versus-exclusive access.
column7: Persistent field-sensitive CFG loans cover local and cross-call reference paths through last use.
column9: Safe caller-owned subset.

column6: Static, untracked, unsafe, inferred, union, and projected origins describe reference provenance.
column7: Named, inferred, union, and projected caller-owned origins run; static/untracked/unsafe forms are deferred.

column6: Closures use explicit capture lists and cannot outlive their defining scope.
column7: Selected nested defs and implicit downward captures run; current capture-list syntax and general non-escaping callable environments remain incomplete.
column9: Escaping closures are not a current Mojo parity target.

column6: Initializers, named results, and definite initialization establish every field before normal return.
column7: Fieldwise and handwritten construction, named results, raising initializers, and branch/exception-sensitive out-self definite initialization run.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Unified `__init__(..., copy=)` and `__init__(..., move=)` methods control explicit copying and transfer.
column7: Both unified initializer shapes drive copyability, move behavior, and value semantics through normalized lifecycle symbols.
column9: Legacy initializer spellings are still accepted as a compatibility extension pending source migration.

column6: Destruction follows ASAP lifetime rules and supports explicit-destroy types and raising paths.
column7: ASAP drops, reverse field order, edge cleanup, explicit-destroy obligation checking, named consuming destructors, and raising-destructor fallback paths run.
column9: The obligation checker conservatively rejects partial moves of explicit-destroy aggregates.

column6: `comptime` bindings evaluate compile-time values usable by specialization.
column7: Compile-time Int/type facts and materialized constants run.
column9: Value shapes are narrower.

column6: `comptime if` selects branches and `comptime for` unrolls compile-time iterables.
column7: Both forms elaborate before checking with fuel and unroll limits.
column9: Supported compile-time value shapes are narrower.

column6: Parameters accept types, values, origins, dependent expressions, and variadic packs.
column7: Bounded type, Int/Bool value, and Origin parameters support inference and explicit arguments.
column9: Arbitrary parameter types, dependent expressions, and packs remain open.

column6: Traits declare required methods and compile-time members; structs declare nominal conformance.
column7: Required methods, receiver conventions, nominal conformance, and associated facts run.

column6: Traits refine other traits and may supply inherited default implementations.
column7: Refined requirements and capabilities are inherited; default bodies are statically materialized into conforming structs, with explicit struct methods overriding defaults and ambiguity rejected.
column9: Static materialization avoids runtime trait-object dispatch.

column6: Traits expose associated types and values used by generic code.
column7: Associated compile-time type/value facts support selected protocols.
column9: Full associated-type equality and composition are incomplete.

column6: Parameterized structs may conform conditionally based on parameter capabilities.
column7: Conditional conformance is not implemented.

column6: Generic functions, methods, and structs specialize for concrete type/value arguments.
column7: Type parameters erase in runtime MIR; value parameters participate in identity and selected specialization.
column9: Nested specialization, caching, and complete ordering remain open.

column6: Constraints express compile-time predicates beyond ordinary trait bounds.
column7: Selected trait bounds and type predicates run; general constraint declarations are absent.

column6: Compile-time values materialize into runtime values under defined conversions.
column7: Int, Bool, String, tuple, and selected type facts materialize where explicitly implemented.

column6: Reflection exposes compile-time structure and declaration generation facilities.
column7: Selected type predicates and associated facts exist; general reflection and declaration generation do not.

column6: UnsafePointer supports typed allocation, load/store, arithmetic, provenance, alignment, and explicit deallocation.
column7: VM UnsafePointer supports a narrow allocation/load/store aliasing subset.
column9: Provenance, alignment, deallocation, and typed arithmetic remain open.

column6: SIMD values expose vector lane semantics and lower to CPU vector operations where available.
column7: The VM emulates typed lanes serially with construction, access, mutation, splats, arithmetic, and comparison.
column9: Semantic subset is intended to match; there is no hardware vector lowering.

column6: Indexer and related protocols drive user-defined indexing and slicing.
column7: Concrete dunder indexing and opaque trait-bounded `__getitem__` dispatch run with requirement-derived index/result types.
column9: The complete standard Indexer family and slicing protocols remain open.

column6: `print` and text formatting use Writable and Writer; deprecated Stringable/Representable are not current protocol targets.
column7: Builtin values format intrinsically; user structs must declare Writable or legacy Representable and provide the checked first-pass `__str__() -> String` hook. Bool output matches Mojo capitalization.
column9: The generic Writer API, Writable display/repr operations, and format-spec machinery remain open.

column6: Hashable and Hasher protocols support deterministic and incremental hashing contracts.
column7: Deterministic Hashable dispatch and a self-hosted stateful IncrementalHasher proof run.
column9: The complete standard Hasher byte-writing API remains open.

column6: Layout and LayoutTensor describe CPU/GPU memory layouts and layout-aware access.
column7: No general layout type or LayoutTensor semantics.
column9: GPU-specific portions are excluded; CPU layout semantics remain first-pass work.

column6: GPU kernels, memory spaces, synchronization, and TileTensor execute on accelerators.
column7: GPU execution is intentionally outside first-pass parity.

column6: The language and libraries expose concurrency, parallelism, atomics, tasks, and distributed execution.
column7: Concurrency, parallelism, atomics, tasks, and distributed execution are intentionally excluded.

column6: Mojo imports Python, exposes Mojo to Python, and converts Python object types.
column7: Python interoperability is not a Mojito goal.

column6: Mojo builds and runs native artifacts for configured compilation targets.
column7: Mojito runs checked MIR in a register VM and has no native artifact backend yet.
column9: Cranelift and LLVM are ordered roadmap work.

column6: Compiler feature toggles select experimental language capabilities.
column7: No compatible feature-toggle system.

column6: Mojo provides source debugging and diagnostic tooling.
column7: Mojito provides staged CLI diagnostics but no source debugger.

column6: Mojo includes language-aware test tooling and standard test APIs.
column7: Mojito has Rust and fixture conformance tests but no self-hosted testing package.

column6: Mojo packages and module artifacts support distribution and dependency workflows.
column7: Mojito links source modules from search roots; package artifacts and dependency metadata are open.

column6: Mojo has compiler-owned IR tooling; its internal format is not Mojito's contract.
column7: A stable Mojito-owned textual MIR/VM assembly format is planned but absent.
column9: Required before native backends.

column6: Mojo emits optimized native CPU code through its compiler stack.
column7: Cranelift is the planned first Mojito native CPU backend.

column6: Mojo emits optimized native CPU code through its compiler stack.
column7: LLVM is the planned second Mojito native CPU backend.

column6: No core Mojo parity requirement.
column7: Optional Mojito backend investigation after native CPU backends.

column6: Mojo is implemented on MLIR.
column7: MLIR is an optional stretch backend and not a required Mojito layer.
